mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPIAUCSL index signals persistent inflation that erodes real earnings expectations and leads to tighter future monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the walk-forward Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal's Rank IC is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate increases month-over-month while industrial production simultaneously decreases month-over-month, the joint tightening of monetary policy and contraction of real output creates a stagflationary impulse that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month and INDPRO decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of rising FEDFUNDS and falling INDPRO fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or the interaction term adds no predictive power beyond main effects (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, a month-over-month decline in retail sales signals that restrictive monetary policy is successfully suppressing aggregate demand, amplifying the negative impact on corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4%, RSAFS declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the conditional signal underperforms the unconditional RSAFS signal (difference in Rank IC not significant, p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production of more than 1.0 index point signals an abrupt contraction in manufacturing output that reduces near-term earnings visibility and triggers risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following sharp INDPRO declines are positive in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects strengthening aggregate demand that translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth is followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls decrease month-over-month while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises month-over-month, the joint deterioration signals a broad labor market recession that compresses household income and consumption, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS decreases month-over-month and UNRATE increases month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction effect is not significant (t-test p > 0.05) when added to a model with main effects only.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,300 thousand units signals a collapse in residential construction demand that precedes broader construction and employment weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following HOUST below 1300 are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the signal's Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.
